Package org.rodinp.internal.core

Class MultiOperation

java.lang.Object
org.rodinp.internal.core.RodinDBOperation
org.rodinp.internal.core.MultiOperation
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.eclipse.core.resources.IWorkspaceRunnable, org.eclipse.core.runtime.ICoreRunnable, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor
Direct Known Subclasses:
ClearElementsOperation, CopyElementsOperation, CopyResourceElementsOperation, DeleteElementsOperation, DeleteResourceElementsOperation
public abstract class MultiOperation extends RodinDBOperation
This class is used to perform operations on multiple IRodinElement. It is responsible for running each operation in turn, collecting the errors and merging the corresponding RodinElementDeltas.

If several errors occurred, they are collected in a multi-status RodinDBStatus. Otherwise, a simple RodinDBStatus is thrown.

    Sets up the renamings hashtable - keys are the elements and values are the new name.

    isMove()
    Returns true if this operation represents a move or rename, false if this operation represents a copy.
    Note: a rename is just a move within the same parent with a name change.

    Returns true if this operation represents a rename, false if this operation represents a copy or move.

    Subclasses must implement this method to process a given IRodinElement.

    Processes all the IRodinElements in turn, collecting errors and updating the progress monitor.

    Sets the new names to use for each element being copied.

    This method is called for each IRodinElement before processElement.

    verifyChildType(InternalElement parent, InternalElement element)
     
    protected void
    verifyDestination(IRodinElement element, IRodinElement destination)
    Verifies that the destination specified for the element is valid for the types of the element and destination.
    protected void
    verifyRenaming(IRodinElement element)
    Verify that the new name specified for element is valid for that type of Rodin element.
    protected void
    verifySibling(IRodinElement element, IRodinElement destination)
    Verifies that the positioning sibling specified for the element exists and its parent is the destination container of this element.

  • Field Details

    • insertBeforeElements

      protected Map<IRodinElement,IRodinElement> insertBeforeElements
      Table specifying insertion positions for elements being copied/moved/renamed. Keyed by elements being processed, and values are the corresponding insertion point.
      See Also:
      • processElements()

    • newParents

      private Map<IRodinElement,IRodinElement> newParents
      Table specifying the new parent for elements being copied/moved/renamed. Keyed by elements being processed, and values are the corresponding destination parent.

    • renamings

      protected Map<IRodinElement,String> renamings
      This table presents the data in renamingList in a more convenient way.

    • renamingsList

      protected String[] renamingsList
      The list of renamings supplied to the operation

    • deltasPerProject

      protected Map<IRodinProject,RodinElementDelta> deltasPerProject
      Table specifying deltas for resources being copied/moved/renamed/deleted. Keyed by elements' project(s), and values are the corresponding deltas.

  • Method Details

    • error

      protected void error(int code, IRodinElement element) throws RodinDBException
      Convenience method to create a RodinDBException embedding a RodinDBStatus.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • executeOperation

      protected void executeOperation() throws RodinDBException
      Executes the operation.
      Specified by:
      executeOperation in class RodinDBOperation
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if one or several errors occurred during the operation. If multiple errors occurred, the corresponding RodinDBStatus is a multi-status. Otherwise, it is a simple one.

    • getDestinationParent

      protected IRodinElement getDestinationParent(IRodinElement child)
      Returns the parent of the element being copied/moved/renamed.

    • getMainTaskName

      protected abstract String getMainTaskName()
      Returns the name to be used by the progress monitor.

    • getNewNameFor

      protected String getNewNameFor(IRodinElement element)
      Returns the new name for element, or null if there are no renamings specified.

    • initializeRenamings

      private void initializeRenamings()
      Sets up the renamings hashtable - keys are the elements and values are the new name.

    • isMove

      protected boolean isMove()
      Returns true if this operation represents a move or rename, false if this operation represents a copy.
      Note: a rename is just a move within the same parent with a name change.

    • isRename

      protected boolean isRename()
      Returns true if this operation represents a rename, false if this operation represents a copy or move.

    • processElement

      protected abstract void processElement(IRodinElement element) throws RodinDBException
      Subclasses must implement this method to process a given IRodinElement.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • processElements

      protected void processElements() throws RodinDBException
      Processes all the IRodinElements in turn, collecting errors and updating the progress monitor.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if one or several operation(s) was unable to be completed.

    • setInsertBefore

      public void setInsertBefore(IRodinElement modifiedElement, IRodinElement newSibling)
      Sets the insertion position in the new container for the modified element. The element being modified will be inserted before the specified new sibling. The given sibling must be a child of the destination container specified for the modified element. The default is null, which indicates that the element is to be inserted at the end of the container.

    • setRenamings

      public void setRenamings(String[] renamingsList)
      Sets the new names to use for each element being copied. The renamings correspond to the elements being processed, and the number of renamings must match the number of elements being processed. A null entry in the list indicates that an element is not to be renamed.

      Note that some renamings may not be used. If both a parent and a child have been selected for copy/move, only the parent is changed. Therefore, if a new name is specified for the child, the child's name will not be changed.

    • verify

      protected abstract void verify(IRodinElement element) throws RodinDBException
      This method is called for each IRodinElement before processElement. It should check that this element can be processed.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • verifyDestination

      protected void verifyDestination(IRodinElement element, IRodinElement destination) throws RodinDBException
      Verifies that the destination specified for the element is valid for the types of the element and destination.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • verifyChildType

      private void verifyChildType(InternalElement parent, InternalElement element) throws RodinDBException
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • verifyRenaming

      protected void verifyRenaming(IRodinElement element) throws RodinDBException
      Verify that the new name specified for element is valid for that type of Rodin element.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • verifySibling

      protected void verifySibling(IRodinElement element, IRodinElement destination) throws RodinDBException
      Verifies that the positioning sibling specified for the element exists and its parent is the destination container of this element.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • getDeltaFor

      protected RodinElementDelta getDeltaFor(IRodinProject rodinProject)
      Returns the RodinElementDelta for rodinProject, creating it and putting it in deltasPerProject if it does not exist yet.
